comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), cross-checked against the full sibling roster.

This child's contribution is the verified-fake honesty layer: it pins the FakeAcliClient's enrichment return shape to the real AcliClient's, so a future real-API change surfaces as fake-drift. That directly contributes to parent SC1 ("Producer (fetcher+enrichment) and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") and to parent AC1's intent (the shared shape is honest at the source), complementing 8937 (schema SSOT + round-trip contract) and fe57 (fixtures + fake). Its AC3 ("structure/type, not exact values — anti-change-detector") applies the anti-change-detector posture to its own tests. No parent criterion is left uncovered *because of this child*; the criteria it does not touch are correctly delegated to named siblings (AC2/SC1→8937; AC3/SC2→33d0; AC4/SC3→2359; AC5/SC4→822a; fixtures/fake foundation→fe57). No coverage finding against child 59ef itself.

One ROSTER-LEVEL absence (not this child's fault, reportable per G3 — already raised on sibling 33d0; restated here for this pairing's record):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests") is only PARTIALLY covered — applied per-test, not owned as a documented governing artifact.
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 59ef AC3 "Assertions check structure/type (keys + JSON primitive types), not exact values (anti-change-detector)" is self-scoped to this story's own tests; 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 822a AC4, 8937 AC4 are likewise self-scoped; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented guardrail/policy governing FUTURE/new reconciler tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: 8937, the contract keystone) or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: this child FULLY contributes to SC1/AC1 (verified-fake honesty); AC6 PARTIALLY covered at roster level (single absence above); all other parent criteria covered by named siblings; AC7 is structural/aggregate. No coverage finding attributable to child 59ef.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × this child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

This child owns the VERIFIED-FAKE fidelity guarantee: a dual-run (fake always + real live-gated) shape-contract harness over the enrichment surface (`get_comment_map`/`get_issuelinks_map`/`get_parent_map`/`search_issues`), so the fake fails the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's. This directly serves the fidelity half of parent SC1 ("Producer and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") — specifically the failure mode "tests pass against fictional shapes" (bug 0ee6's root cause) — and reinforces fe57's "hermetic-but-honest fixtures" and 8937's contract. Its own ACs (59ef AC1 dual-run; AC2 fake fails on shape divergence; AC3 structure/type not exact values; AC4 live opt-in/no flake) are internally coherent and consistent with declared scope (fixtures→fe57, snapshot round-trip→8937 explicitly OUT).

4-bucket per parent criterion (roster-level):
- AC1 (SSOT snapshot-entry schema; producer/consumer validate) — FULLY by 8937 + fe57; 59ef contributes fake-fidelity so the contract stays honest.
- AC2 (one round-trip contract through production path, real fixtures) — FULLY by 8937 + fe57.
- AC3 (collapse dual appliers; delete retired) — FULLY by 33d0.
- AC4 (sub-op telemetry; computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly) — FULLY by 2359.
- AC5 (hermetic bidirectional round-trip; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards) — FULLY by 822a.
- AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests) — PARTIALLY (single roster-level absence below).
- AC7 (all children closed; suite green; no net brittle increase) — structural/aggregate epic roll-up.
- SC1 (no silent producer/consumer shape drift) — FULLY by 8937; 59ef OWNS the fake-fidelity half.
- SC2/SC3/SC4 — FULLY by 33d0 / 2359 / 822a respectively.
- SC5 (semantic round-trip/structure-type; zero change-detector) — posture clause covered; 59ef AC3 contributes.

No per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to child 59ef itself; its owned criteria (verified-fake dual-run shape contract / SC1 fidelity half) are fully and coherently covered.

ONE ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; already raised by Iso Tester on siblings 33d0/2359/822a/8937 — restated here for this pairing's record after re-cross-checking the WHOLE roster including this child):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including this one (59ef AC3), only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests.
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 59ef AC3 "Assertions check structure/type (keys + JSON primitive types), not exact values (anti-change-detector)" is self-scoped to THIS story's own contract tests; 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 822a AC4, 8937 AC4, and fe57 are likewise self-scoped to each story's own tests; no sibling defines/produces a documented guardrail that governs new/future tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: 8937, the contract keystone — e.g. a new AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests") or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

No coverage finding is attributable to child 59ef itself for its owned criteria; the single absence above is a roster-level AC6 gap already tracked on sibling tickets.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57).

OWNED CRITERIA — child 59ef's slice is FULLY covered; no per-criterion coverage gap is attributable to this child:
- Verified-fake FIDELITY half of parent SC1 ("Producer and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") → 59ef AC1 (dual-run shape-contract harness over the enrichment surface get_comment_map/get_issuelinks_map/get_parent_map/search_issues, fake always + real live-gated) + AC2 (fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's). This pins the specific bug-0ee6 failure mode "tests pass against fictional shapes," complementing 8937 (schema SSOT + joint round-trip) and fe57 (honest fixtures/fake foundation). FULLY.
- Contributes to AC1 intent (the shared snapshot shape stays honest at the producer/enrichment source) and to SC5/AC6 posture via 59ef AC3 (assertions check structure/type — keys + JSON primitive types — not exact values; anti-change-detector) and AC4 (live opt-in, no flake/slowdown of default suite).

DELEGATION — criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; declared OUT items (fixtures → story A/fe57; snapshot round-trip → story B/8937) are consistent with the roster:
- AC1 schema SSOT + AC2 round-trip contract / SC1 → 8937 (keystone) + fe57 (real-payload fixtures + fake foundation); 59ef adds the fidelity guarantee.
- AC3/SC2 (collapse dual outbound appliers) → 33d0.
- AC4/SC3 (sub-op telemetry; computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly) → 2359.
- AC5/SC4 (hermetic bidirectional round-trip; de-encode 'expected-bug' probe guards) → 822a.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: SC1 (verified-fake fidelity half) FULLY by THIS child; AC1 contributed (schema artifact → 8937); AC2/AC3/AC4/AC5/SC2/SC3/SC4 covered by named siblings; SC5 posture clause applied per-test (incl. 59ef AC3); AC7 structural/aggregate epic roll-up; AC6 PARTIALLY covered (single roster-level absence, below).

ROSTER-LEVEL absence (NOT this child's fault; ALREADY tracked across siblings 2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57 AND on this ticket via two prior G3 audits): Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests — no mock-call-count, no whole-blob golden equality; assert semantic round-trip + structure/type") is only PARTIALLY covered. Every child, including 59ef (AC3), only APPLIES the anti-change-detector posture to its OWN tests; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented governing guardrail/policy artifact that governs FUTURE/new reconciler tests (AC6's operative verb is "governs new tests"). Repo cross-check confirms docs/mutation-testing.md is a general mutation-testing doc, not the AC6 reconciler-test governing artifact, and no child references producing/extending it. Natural home repeatedly named across the roster: keystone 8937 (e.g. a new AC "a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests"), or a small dedicated child.

This single AC6 gap is NOT re-filed as a new structured finding here — it is fully recorded on this ticket and across the sibling set; re-emitting it a seventh time would be duplicative. No NEW coverage finding for this pairing: child 59ef's owned criteria (verified-fake dual-run shape contract / SC1 fidelity half) are FULLY covered, and the only roster-level gap (AC6) is already tracked.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: G3 (parent f89d × child 59ef) — owned-criteria verdict: this child's slice (verified-fake dual-run shape-contract harness = SC1 fidelity half / contributes AC1 honesty) is FULLY covered and consistent with its declared scope (fixtures→fe57, snapshot round-trip→8937 explicitly OUT). No coverage gap attributable to child 59ef itself. Per the prior audits on this ticket and across the roster, the only roster-level gap remains parent AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests): every child including 59ef AC3 only applies the posture to its own tests; no sibling produces the documented governing artifact. Re-emitting structured finding for this pairing's record per G3; AC6's natural home remains keystone 8937 or a small dedicated child.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. Each of the 7 cross-child interaction modes was checked; NO high-confidence finding is attributable to this pairing. Clean pairing (empty findings).

Repo corroboration: the enrichment surface 59ef puts under contract — get_comment_map (acli_graph.py L289), get_issuelinks_map (L391), get_parent_map, search_issues — all exist on the real AcliClient and return the raw REST-nested field shapes (comment field dict {"comments":[...]}; issuelinks list with type.name + inward/outwardIssue), exactly what fetcher.fetch_snapshot merges (fetcher.py L413-417, L450-454). The shapes 59ef asserts are real, and the methods are not moved/renamed by any sibling.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 59ef depends_on fe57 and exercises fe57's FakeAcliClient (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py) plus the real AcliClient (acli_graph.py). fe57 BUILDS the fake; 59ef VERIFIES it. Clean producer/verifier split with a declared dependency edge; no conflicting writes to a shared file (59ef adds test_acliclient_contract.py and only reads acli_graph.py).
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("FakeAcliClient serves fixtures through the production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") and 59ef AC2 ("the fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") are mutually reinforcing, not contradictory: a fixture-faithful, unmassaged fake is precisely what passes 59ef's shape contract. No count-vs-list / representation mismatch on the inbound enrichment surface (unlike the outbound labels_applied issue on 2359/33d0, which 59ef does not touch).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 59ef correctly declares depends_on fe57 (needs the fake to run the hermetic leg). It correctly scopes the snapshot round-trip OUT (→ 8937) and fixture capture OUT (→ fe57); it needs neither 8937's schema nor 822a's probe work to stand up its dual-run harness. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 59ef asserts the enrichment METHODS' return shape (client surface); 8937 asserts the assembled SNAPSHOT-ENTRY schema after fetch; 822a asserts inbound↔outbound ROUND-TRIP behavior. Three distinct surfaces exercising fidelity from different angles — complementary, not duplicative.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only ordering constraint (fake before its verifier) is captured by the declared depends_on fe57. No unsequenced same-region co-edit (contrast the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier shared-state cluster, which involves neither 59ef's files nor its surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The dual-run harness adds a live-gated leg; 59ef AC4 makes live opt-in and explicitly guards against flaking/slowing the default suite (consistent with parent AC7 "no net increase in brittle tests"), and AC3 asserts structure/type not exact values, so it does not impose brittle expectations on any consumer.
- Residual references — BENIGN. All four referenced methods exist in acli_graph.py; the referenced fake is the artifact fe57 produces. 33d0 collapses the OUTBOUND applier paths (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py), not the INBOUND enrichment methods 59ef contracts, so 33d0's deletion strands nothing here. No dangling reference.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 coverage matter already tracked across the sibling set and on this ticket's prior G3 audits — it is not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 59ef): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence/benign-reading filter.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 finding.

Repo corroboration: the four enrichment methods 59ef puts under contract all resolve on the real AcliClient via MRO — AcliClient(AcliRestMixin, AcliGraphMixin) (acli.py L221); search_issues on the Rest side (acli.py L370); get_parent_map (acli_graph.py L189), get_comment_map (L289), get_issuelinks_map (L391) on the Graph mixin. The dual-run premise (one client surface, contract both real + fake) is sound; no sibling moves/renames these methods.

7-mode walk:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 59ef depends_on fe57 and exercises fe57's FakeAcliClient (_fakes.py) plus the real AcliClient; fe57 BUILDS the fake, 59ef VERIFIES it. 59ef adds tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_acliclient_contract.py and only reads acli_graph.py — no shared-file co-edit.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake serves fixtures through production enrichment with NO shape transformation") and 59ef AC2 ("fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") are mutually reinforcing — an unmassaged, fixture-faithful fake is exactly what passes 59ef's shape contract.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 59ef correctly declares depends_on fe57 (needs the fake for the hermetic leg); snapshot round-trip (→8937) and fixture capture (→fe57) are correctly OUT; it needs neither 8937's schema nor 822a's probe to stand up its harness.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 59ef contracts the enrichment METHODS' return shape (client surface); 8937 contracts the assembled SNAPSHOT-ENTRY schema; 822a asserts inbound↔outbound ROUND-TRIP. Three complementary angles, not duplicative.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. Only ordering constraint (fake before verifier) is captured by the declared depends_on fe57. No unsequenced same-region co-edit (the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier cluster touches neither 59ef's files nor its surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 59ef AC4 makes the live leg opt-in and guards against flaking/slowing the default suite (consistent with parent AC7 "no net increase in brittle tests"); AC3 asserts structure/type not exact values, imposing no brittle expectation on consumers.
- Residual references — BENIGN. All four methods exist on the real client; 33d0 collapses the OUTBOUND applier (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), not the INBOUND enrichment surface 59ef contracts, so its deletion strands nothing here.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 coverage matter already tracked across the sibling set and this ticket's prior audits — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not re-filed here.

No G4 cross-child finding for this pairing; all 7 modes read benign.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion T3 — Integration feasibility [overlay]).

AFFIRMATIONS (T3 sub-checks that PASS, tool-grounded):
- (a) technical_feasibility / (b) endpoints+methods MATCH: every external surface the epic depends on exists in the production client and is already used — verified against the repo:
  - search_issues → acli.py:370; get_comment_map → acli_graph.py:289; get_issuelinks_map → acli_graph.py:391; get_parent_map → acli_graph.py:189 (the exact signatures `(project, jql=None)` 59ef's dual-run harness contracts).
  - The underlying `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` endpoint is live in production (acli_graph.py:236/337/431 via `_direct_rest_post_json`). MATCH, not asserted-but-unverified.
- (c) auth/HTTPS preconditions STATED and grounded: HTTPS Basic-auth over the configured `jira_url` with stored api_token (acli_rest.py:243-256). The live leg's gate is real: the `external` pytest marker + `REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL=1` opt-in + per-test credential skipif already exist (tests/external/conftest.py:26-41; tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py:65-70). 59ef AC1/AC4 ("live-gated marker", "live opt-in, no flake/slowdown") correctly reuse this existing, working precondition.
- Overall: the inbound-enrichment integration (and story A's live capture script) is an INTERNAL, already-used integration — T3 reads as PASS for the bulk of the epic.

ONE T3 COACHING ITEM (the only part that asserts an external capability NOT used before):
- [ ] Confirm Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI spec actually covers the nested fields you intend to validate (`comment`/`comments`, `issuelinks`, `parent`) before relying on the optional spec-validation, OR keep it explicitly behind a SPIKE.
  - criteria: T3
  - location: story 59ef "What" → "Optional: validate the real client's responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI where the spec covers the field (investigate spec completeness first — see epic open question)"
  - evidence: This is the one externally-new capability in the epic (the repo has no existing OpenAPI/spec-validation usage — repo search for "OpenAPI" returns no client-side validator). Per T3(d), a capability gap should route to a SPIKE before commitment. The plan ALREADY does the right thing — it marks the item "Optional", caveats "investigate spec completeness first", and defers to an "epic open question" — so this is a confirm-and-keep-gated nudge, not a blocker. The load-bearing unknown is whether the public spec models those specific nested REST shapes (historically thin in Jira's OpenAPI); if it does not, the optional path yields no signal and should be dropped rather than half-built.
  - suggested_fix: (left empty — outcome depends on the spike's finding about spec coverage, which I can't determine from the plan text)

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 finding attributable to this child, and NO uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child:
- Parent SC "Producer (fetcher+enrichment) and consumers cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing" — the FIDELITY half (the bug-0ee6 failure mode "tests pass against fictional shapes") → 59ef AC1 (dual-run shape-contract harness over the enrichment surface get_comment_map/get_issuelinks_map/get_parent_map/search_issues; fake always-hermetic + real live-gated) + AC2 (the fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's). FULLY. This complements 8937 (schema SSOT + joint round-trip) and fe57 (honest fixtures/fake foundation), which own the producer↔consumer-drift half via the snapshot contract.
- CONTRIBUTED: parent SC "new tests assert behavior/contract … zero change-detector tests added" via 59ef AC3 (assertions check structure/type — keys + JSON primitive types — not exact values); and parent SC "suite stays lean / no flake" via 59ef AC4 (live opt-in; does not flake or slow the default suite).

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; this child's declared OUT items (capturing fixtures → story A/fe57; snapshot round-trip → story B/8937) are consistent with the roster:
- AC "SSOT snapshot-entry schema module" + AC "test validates fetcher snapshot vs schema" + AC "test validates each differ's reads vs schema" + AC "one round-trip contract test through the production path using real-Jira fixtures" → 8937 (AC1/AC2/AC3) + fe57 (real-payload fixtures/fake).
- AC "outbound collapsed to a single production path" + AC "retired outbound applier path deleted" → 33d0 (AC1).
- AC "outbound sub-op telemetry surfaced in the structured batch outcome" + AC "computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly (warn-first)" → 2359 (AC1/AC2/AC3).
- AC "bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically" + AC "every probe assertion encoding a current bug as expected converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes" → 822a (AC1/AC2).

ANTI-FP — the recurring "Parent AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) only PARTIALLY covered" finding from earlier audits on this ticket is a FALSE POSITIVE and is WITHDRAWN. The actual parent acceptance criterion reads verbatim: "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)." It (a) explicitly NAMES its owner — story B = sibling 8937 — and (b) is FULLY covered there: 8937 description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap): commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests — a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it," and 8937 AC5 = "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note recording the testing-strategy decision + the schema location + the rules) is committed and referenced by the new tests." Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a named consumer counts), this is FULLY covered, not partial. The earlier "AC6 PARTIALLY" finding was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text and predates 8937's explicit ownership; it is not re-filed. (The most recent G3 audits on siblings 33d0 and 2359 already withdrew the same false positive.)

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY — parent SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on shape" (fidelity half OWNED by this child); CONTRIBUTED — parent SC "zero change-detector" (59ef AC3) + parent SC "suite stays lean / no flake" (59ef AC4); COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — all schema/contract/round-trip (8937+fe57), applier-collapse (33d0), telemetry/canary (2359), bidirectional round-trip/probe-de-encode (822a), and the documented guardrail (8937 AC5, explicitly named owner); STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green." UNCOVERED — none. No G3 coverage finding for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 finding.

Repo corroboration of 59ef's premise (the dual-run surface is real, not fictional): the four methods 59ef's harness contracts all resolve on the real client with the exact signatures referenced — search_issues(jql, start_at=0, max_results=50) (acli.py:370-375), get_comment_map(project, jql=None) (acli_graph.py:289-293), get_issuelinks_map (acli_graph.py:391), get_parent_map (acli_graph.py:189). get_comment_map returns the RAW nested Jira `comment` field {"comments":[...], "total":N} (docstring acli_graph.py:303-308) — the exact bug-0ee6 seam — so contracting the fake's return shape against the real client's is grounded. No sibling moves/renames these methods.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 59ef adds tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_acliclient_contract.py and only READS acli_graph.py; it CONSUMES fe57's FakeAcliClient (_fakes.py) and the real AcliClient. fe57 BUILDS the fake, 59ef VERIFIES it — clean producer/verifier split over the declared depends_on fe57 edge; no shared mutable write.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") and 59ef AC2 ("fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") are mutually reinforcing — an unmassaged, fixture-faithful fake is precisely what passes 59ef's real-vs-fake shape contract. No representation mismatch on the inbound enrichment surface (the outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry matter tracked on 2359/33d0 is outbound-internal and untouched here).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 59ef correctly declares depends_on fe57 (needs the fake for the hermetic leg). Snapshot round-trip (→8937) and fixture capture (→fe57) are correctly scoped OUT; the harness stands up without 8937's schema or 822a's probe. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 59ef contracts the enrichment METHODS' return shape (client surface); 8937 contracts the assembled SNAPSHOT-ENTRY schema; 822a asserts inbound↔outbound ROUND-TRIP behavior. Three distinct surfaces — complementary, not duplicative.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only ordering constraint (fake before its verifier) is captured by the declared depends_on fe57. 59ef is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier same-region sequencing cluster (different files/surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 59ef AC4 makes the live leg opt-in and explicitly guards against flaking/slowing the default suite (consistent with parent SC "suite stays lean"); AC3 asserts structure/type not exact values, imposing no brittle expectation on any consumer.
- Residual references — BENIGN. All four contracted methods exist on the real client (verified above); the referenced fake is fe57's artifact. 33d0 collapses the OUTBOUND applier (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py), NOT the INBOUND enrichment surface 59ef contracts, so its deletion strands nothing here. No dangling reference.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) is a G3 COVERAGE matter, not a G4 cross-child inconsistency — and per the plan's verbatim AC it is explicitly owned by story B (8937), so it is neither re-filed nor a G4 issue here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 59ef): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 coverage finding attributable to this child; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED / CONTRIBUTED by THIS child (59ef = the verified-fake honesty check: dual-run shape-contract harness over the AcliClient enrichment surface):
- Parent SC "Producer (fetcher + enrichment) and consumers (differs/appliers) cannot drift on snapshot shape without a test failing" → 59ef OWNS the FAKE-FIDELITY half: its dual-run shape-contract tests (get_comment_map / get_issuelinks_map / get_parent_map / search_issues) run against BOTH the real AcliClient (live-gated) and the FakeAcliClient, and the fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's (59ef AC1/AC2). This is the Google "verified fake" guarantee that keeps fe57's fixtures/fake honest, so the snapshot round-trip (8937) and bidirectional round-trip (822a) cannot pass against fictional shapes — the exact bug-0ee6 failure mode. CONTRIBUTED (fidelity half), complementary to fe57 (honest fixtures) and 8937 (joint round-trip / schema SSOT).
- Parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract (semantic round-trip, structure/type); the suite stays lean; zero change-detector tests added" → 59ef AC3 (assertions check structure/type — keys + JSON primitive types — not exact values) + AC4 (live run opt-in; does not flake or slow the default suite). CONTRIBUTED.
- Parent AC "round-trip contract test through the production path using fixtures derived from real Jira payloads" → 59ef reinforces the FIDELITY of the fake that feeds that round-trip; the round-trip test itself is correctly delegated to 8937 and the fixtures/fake to fe57 (59ef declares depends_on fe57; scopes "capturing the fixtures (story A)" and "the snapshot round-trip (story B)" explicitly OUT). Consistent with the roster.

DELEGATION — parent criteria 59ef does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; 59ef's declared OUT items (fixtures → story A/fe57; snapshot round-trip → story B/8937) are consistent with the roster:
- AC "SSOT snapshot-entry schema module" + AC "fetcher snapshot validated vs schema" + AC "each differ's reads validated vs schema" + AC "one round-trip contract test through the production path using real-Jira fixtures" → 8937 (AC1/AC2/AC3) + fe57 (real-payload fixtures/fake foundation).
- AC "Outbound collapsed to a single production path" + AC "retired outbound applier path deleted" + SC "outbound exercised through the production path, not a dead path" → 33d0.
- AC "outbound sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) surfaced" + AC "computed > 0 && applied == 0 fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout" + SC "silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary" → 2359.
- AC "bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically through the production path" + AC "every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded" + SC "no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a (depends_on fe57).
- AC "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" → 8937 (story B): 8937 description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact … commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail … a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it" + 8937 AC5. The parent AC NAMES its owner verbatim; under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts) this is FULLY covered.

ANTI-FP / WITHDRAWAL: the recurring "Parent AC6 documented anti-change-detector guardrail only PARTIALLY covered — no sibling produces a governing artifact" finding raised on earlier audits across the roster (incl. this ticket's prior audits via 59ef AC3 being "self-scoped") is a FALSE POSITIVE and stays WITHDRAWN. It was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text; the actual parent AC reads verbatim "… committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" — it explicitly names story B (= 8937) as owner and is delivered by 8937's description + AC5. Sibling audits on 2359/33d0/822a have already withdrawn the same false positive.

4-bucket summary for this pairing:
- CONTRIBUTED (59ef owns its fidelity half): parent SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on snapshot shape" (verified-fake fidelity half); parent SC "zero change-detector / lean suite" (structure/type assertions, opt-in live run).
- COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING: SSOT schema + fetcher/differ validation + round-trip contract (8937 + fe57); applier collapse + retired-path deletion + production-path exercise (33d0); sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary (2359); bidirectional hermetic round-trip + probe de-encoding (822a); real-payload fixtures/fake foundation (fe57); documented anti-change-detector guardrail (8937, story B — named owner).
- STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE: parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green."
- UNCOVERED: none.

No G3 coverage finding for this pairing. 59ef's owned share (the dual-run real+fake shape-contract harness that makes the fake fail when its return shape diverges from the real client) is a coherent, fully-scoped contributor to the no-drift guarantee; it owns no parent acceptance criterion solely and leaves none uncovered. The union of children covers every parent acceptance/success criterion.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, corroborated against the repo. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

Repo corroboration of 59ef's premise (the dual-run surface is real, not fictional): all four enrichment methods 59ef's harness contracts resolve on the real AcliClient with the exact referenced signatures — search_issues(jql, start_at=0, max_results=50) (acli.py:370-375), get_comment_map(project, jql=None) (acli_graph.py:289-293), get_issuelinks_map (acli_graph.py:391), get_parent_map (acli_graph.py:189). get_comment_map returns the RAW nested Jira `comment` field {"comments":[...], "total":N} (docstring acli_graph.py:303-308) — the exact bug-0ee6 seam — so contracting the fake's return shape against the real client's is grounded. Existing production-path fakes already use these signatures (tests/unit/.../mutate/test_comment_enrichment.py:196, test_link_sync.py:163), confirming the surface is real and in use. No sibling moves/renames these methods.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 59ef adds tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_acliclient_contract.py and only READS acli_graph.py; it CONSUMES fe57's FakeAcliClient (_fakes.py) and the real AcliClient. fe57 BUILDS the fake, 59ef VERIFIES it — clean producer/verifier split over the declared depends_on fe57 edge; no shared mutable write.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("fake serves fixtures through production fetch_snapshot enrichment with NO shape transformation") and 59ef AC2 ("fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") are mutually reinforcing — an unmassaged, fixture-faithful fake is precisely what passes 59ef's real-vs-fake shape contract. No representation mismatch on the inbound enrichment surface (the outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry matter on 2359/33d0 is outbound-internal and untouched here).
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 59ef correctly declares depends_on fe57 (needs the fake for the hermetic leg). Fixture capture (→fe57) and snapshot round-trip (→8937) are correctly scoped OUT; the harness stands up without 8937's schema or 822a's probe. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 59ef contracts the enrichment METHODS' return shape (client surface); 8937 contracts the assembled SNAPSHOT-ENTRY schema; 822a asserts inbound↔outbound ROUND-TRIP behavior. Three distinct, complementary surfaces.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only ordering constraint (fake before its verifier) is captured by depends_on fe57. 59ef is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier same-region sequencing cluster (different files/surface).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 59ef AC4 makes the live leg opt-in and guards against flaking/slowing the default suite (consistent with parent SC "suite stays lean"); AC3 asserts structure/type not exact values, imposing no brittle expectation on any consumer.
- Residual references — BENIGN. All four contracted methods exist on the real client (verified above); the referenced fake is fe57's artifact. 33d0 collapses the OUTBOUND applier (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), NOT the INBOUND enrichment surface 59ef contracts, so its deletion strands nothing here.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) is a G3 COVERAGE matter — and per the plan's verbatim AC it is explicitly owned by story B (8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 59ef): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/822a/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding (empty findings list).

Setup facts grounding the walk: 59ef declares depends_on fe57 (link present). Its file_impact is test_acliclient_contract.py (new) + acli_graph.py (enrichment methods under contract, read-only). It CONSUMES fe57's FakeAcliClient and the real AcliClient; it explicitly scopes OUT fixture capture (→fe57) and the snapshot round-trip (→8937), consistent with the roster.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 59ef adds a net-new test file and only reads acli_graph.py. The FakeAcliClient is PRODUCED by fe57 and VERIFIED (consumed) by 59ef — a clean producer/verifier split over the declared depends_on fe57 edge; no shared mutable write co-edited with any sibling (fe57 owns _fakes.py; 8937 owns _snapshot_schema.py/fetcher.py; 33d0/2359 own the outbound applier files; 822a owns the probe/round-trip files).
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. fe57 AC2 ("FakeAcliClient serves fixtures through the production enrichment with NO shape transformation") and 59ef AC2 ("the fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") are mutually reinforcing — an unmassaged, fixture-faithful fake is exactly what passes 59ef's real-vs-fake shape contract. The outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry matter tracked on the 2359↔33d0 pairing is outbound-internal; 59ef contracts only the INBOUND enrichment surface and neither asserts nor depends on it.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 59ef correctly declares depends_on fe57 (needs the fake to run the hermetic leg). Its harness stands up without 8937's schema or 822a's probe; OUT items (fixtures→fe57, round-trip→8937) are present as named siblings. No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 59ef contracts the enrichment METHODS' return shape (client surface); 8937 contracts the assembled SNAPSHOT-ENTRY schema after fetch; 822a asserts inbound↔outbound ROUND-TRIP behavior. Three distinct, complementary surfaces — not duplicative; 59ef declares the others OUT consistently.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. The only hard ordering edge (the fake must exist before its verifier) is captured by the declared depends_on fe57. 59ef is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier same-region sequencing cluster (different files/surface entirely).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 59ef AC4 makes the live leg opt-in (existing live/external marker) and guards against flaking/slowing the default suite, consistent with parent SC "suite stays lean"; AC3 asserts structure/type not exact values, imposing no brittle expectation on any consumer. The "Optional: validate against Jira Cloud OpenAPI" item is explicitly gated/spiked, so it constrains no sibling.
- Residual references — BENIGN. The four enrichment methods 59ef contracts (get_comment_map, get_issuelinks_map, get_parent_map, search_issues) are live on the real AcliClient and not moved/renamed by any sibling; the referenced fake is fe57's artifact. 33d0's collapse/deletion targets the OUTBOUND applier path, NOT the INBOUND enrichment surface 59ef contracts, so its deletion strands nothing here. No dangling reference.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail criterion is a G3 COVERAGE matter — and per the plan's verbatim AC it is explicitly owned by story B (8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 59ef): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — G6 (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection). One affirmation-heavy chunk with one minor coaching finding.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 PASSES):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the proposed dual-run parametrized harness is the canonical "verified fake" mechanism and is data-flow-complete against the actual code. The real client's enrichment surface (`acli_graph.get_comment_map`/`get_parent_map`, `acli.search_issues`) and the existing `FakeAcliClient` (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py) expose IDENTICAL signatures — `get_comment_map(project, jql=None)`, `get_parent_map(project, jql=None)`, `search_issues(jql, start_at, max_results)` — so a single parametrized contract-test body genuinely runs against both. The "fake fails if shape diverges" claim is achievable because the fake returns fixture payloads verbatim with no shape massaging.
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: the approach solves the NAMED problem (fake-drift reintroducing bug 0ee6's fictional-shape failure), not a proxy. Shape/type assertions (keys + JSON primitive types, not exact values) are the correct anti-change-detector design and directly target the nested-`comment` vs flat-`comments` / absent-`issuelinks` class of bug.
- LIVE GATING: opt-in via the existing `external` marker is already an established codebase convention (tests/external/conftest.py gates on REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL + live Jira creds; test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py / test_reconcile_live.py follow it), so AC4 ("does not flake or slow the default suite") rests on a real, proven seam.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE: the "Why" section states an explicit, well-grounded positive rationale (verified-fake pattern from SWE@Google; prevents bug-0ee6 drift) — G6 item (4) satisfied.
- APPROACH SELECTION: I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) a one-directional snapshot/golden-file assertion of the real client's shape (no shared test body) and (b) pure OpenAPI-schema validation of real responses. Both are strictly weaker: (a) cannot detect fake drift (the named problem) because the fake is never exercised by the same assertions; (b) depends on Jira Cloud spec completeness (an open question the plan itself flags) and still wouldn't pin the FAKE. The chosen shared-contract-body approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on testability and robustness. No coaching finding — alternatives discarded.

COACHING FINDING (minor):
- [ ] Make the "fake fails on divergence" mechanism non-vacuous: state HOW the live-real shape becomes available to the assertion when live is OFF (the default). criteria=[G6]. The harness runs the real client only behind the opt-in live marker, but AC2 ("the fake fails the contract test if its return shape diverges from the real client's") must hold in the DEFAULT (hermetic) suite to actually prevent drift — otherwise drift is only caught on the rare opt-in live run, weakening fitness-for-purpose. Today the fake returns fixtures captured FROM the real client (tests/fixtures/jira/_meta.json: "Scrubbed real Jira REST payloads captured via the production AcliClient methods"), so the same shape-contract test body asserting on the fake's fixture-derived output already exercises real-captured shapes hermetically. The plan should make explicit that the contract assertions run against the fake's fixture output in the default suite (so divergence surfaces hermetically), and the live run is an additional freshness check that the fixtures still match live Jira — not the only place AC2 is enforced. location: "What" bullet 3 ("The fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") and AC line 2.
  evidence: ["plan AC2: 'The fake fails the contract test if its return shape diverges from the real client's return shape.'", "real client only runs behind opt-in live/external marker per 'What' bullet 2 and AC4", "tests/fixtures/jira/_meta.json: fixtures are real captured payloads — the hermetic divergence signal already exists but the plan does not name it as the default-suite enforcement point"]

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — [T3] Integration feasibility (overlay, applies: plan exercises the real `AcliClient` against live Jira + references Jira Cloud OpenAPI).

VERDICT: PASS with one minor coaching item.

Affirmations (criteria satisfied):
- technical_feasibility: The dual-run shape-contract harness is achievable as described. The live-gating mechanism it depends on already exists in-repo (`tests/external/test_link_sync_live.py`, `tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`) using the `pytest.mark.external` marker + a `_live_jira_ready()` creds/binary gate — the exact "opt-in, does not flake/slow the default suite" pattern AC4 calls for.
- Named-method existence — MATCH: all four enrichment methods exist on the real client. `get_comment_map`/`get_issuelinks_map` at src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_graph.py:289 and :391, with documented return shapes (`{jira_key: <comment field dict>}`, `{jira_key: [<issuelink>, ...]}`); `get_parent_map` and `search_issues` referenced alongside them (acli_graph.py:409 mirror note; search at acli.py:1021+). The real endpoint is `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql` (acli_graph.py:337) — a real Jira Cloud endpoint. Asserting RETURN SHAPE (keys + JSON primitive types) is feasible against these dict/list return types.
- auth/HTTPS preconditions: implicitly satisfied — the live run rides the existing external-marker client builder which constructs `AcliClient(jira_url, user, api_token, jira_project)` from env (test_link_sync_live.py:42-61); HTTPS is the transport of the REST POST.

Finding (one):
- [ ] The "Optional: validate real-client responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI" bullet asserts an external capability whose feasibility is unknown (the plan itself defers spec-completeness to an epic open question). Name this as an explicit SPIKE (investigate-before-commit) rather than an inline Optional bullet, so the core AC1–AC4 harness is not blocked on, or conflated with, an unverified OpenAPI-coverage dependency.
  - criteria: T3
  - location: Plan §What, bullet 4 ("Optional: validate the real client's responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI …")
  - evidence: Plan text "investigate spec completeness first — see epic open question" — an asserted external integration (OpenAPI validation) whose viability is admittedly unknown; routing a capability-gap to a SPIKE is the T3(d) prescription.
  - suggested_fix: Carve the OpenAPI-validation work into a separate SPIKE ticket ("Does Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI cover the comment/issuelinks/parent/search fields well enough to validate response shape?"), and keep this ticket's ACs scoped to the real+fake dual-run harness, which is fully feasible today.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 review (criterion T8 — LLM/prompt structural-completeness overlay): NOT APPLICABLE / PASS. This plan defines a verified-fake contract-testing harness for the Jira `AcliClient` (a subprocess/REST client) — it contains no LLM/agent system: no prompts, sub-agents, reviewers, LLM output schemas, or enum value-vocabularies. The "shape-contract" assertions are about JSON primitive types and key presence on Jira REST payloads, not an LLM output schema. The overlay's probe targets (undefined-but-referenced enum vocabulary, decision-rule co-location, ambiguous counter placement, sub-step fallbacks, instruction-locality/pink-elephant antipatterns) have no surface here. Tool-verified the referenced surface exists and is fully specified (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py:30 `FakeAcliClient` with the four enrichment methods get_comment_map/get_issuelinks_map/get_parent_map/search_issues). No T8 findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion T1 — Prior-art / novel-architecture justification [overlay]). VERDICT: PASS — clean chunk, no findings.

The plan crosses the "novel architectural pattern" bright-line (it adopts a named test-design pattern), so T1 applies. All three sub-checks pass:

AFFIRMATIONS:
- (a) Relevant prior art is CONSIDERED, not reinvented/repackaged. The plan explicitly cites the canonical source by name — Google's "Software Engineering at Google", the **verified fake** — and applies its exact prescription verbatim: "the fake carries contract tests written against the API's public interface, and those same tests run against BOTH the real implementation and the fake." This is adoption of an established pattern, the opposite of the anti-pattern T1 guards against. Repo corroboration: the codebase already calls this same construct a "verified-fake" (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py:1 module docstring "Verified-fake Jira client…"), confirming the team is extending a known, in-use pattern rather than inventing one.
- (b) Novelty is justified vs. an established approach — N/A by virtue of there being NO novelty: this is a well-trodden, textbook pattern (Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging does not fire). No bespoke architecture is proposed; the dual-run parametrized harness over a single client surface is the standard verified-fake shape.
- (c) Unverified capability assertions are RESOLVED. The single externally-new capability assertion in the plan — "validate the real client's responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI where the spec covers the field" — is already self-gated by the plan text: marked "Optional", caveated "investigate spec completeness first", and deferred to the epic open question. Per the pass's DECISIVENESS rule, a plan whose own text affirmatively gates/defers its one unverified capability assertion PASSES; raising a finding here would be a false positive against a well-specified plan. (This aligns with the prior T3 audit, which likewise reads the OpenAPI item as correctly spiked/gated.)

No T1 finding. Empty findings list for this criterion is the correct, expected outcome for this chunk.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — G6 (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection). VERDICT: substantially a PASS — one MINOR coaching finding (missing-mechanism-detail / fitness gap), no critical/major.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria satisfied):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: the dual-run parametrized harness is the canonical "verified fake" mechanism and is data-flow-complete against the actual code. The real client's enrichment surface and the existing fake expose IDENTICAL signatures — get_comment_map(project, jql=None) (acli_graph.py:289) / get_issuelinks_map (acli_graph.py:391) / get_parent_map / search_issues(jql, start_at=0, max_results=50) — and the existing FakeAcliClient (tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py:30-62) returns fixture payloads verbatim with NO shape massaging, so a single parametrized contract body genuinely runs against both and "fake fails on divergence" is achievable.
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: solves the NAMED problem (fake-drift reintroducing bug-0ee6's fictional-shape failure), not a proxy. Shape/type assertions (keys + JSON primitive types, not exact values) directly target the nested-`comment` vs flat-`comments` / absent-`issuelinks` bug class; get_comment_map returns the raw nested {"comments":[...], "total":N} field (acli_graph.py:303-308) — exactly the 0ee6 seam.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present (G6 item 4): the "Why" states an explicit, grounded rationale (verified-fake pattern from SWE@Google; prevents 0ee6 drift).
- APPROACH SELECTION: I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) one-directional snapshot/golden assertion of the real client's shape with no shared test body, and (b) pure OpenAPI-schema validation of real responses. Both are strictly weaker: (a) never exercises the fake under the same assertions so it cannot catch fake drift (the named problem); (b) depends on Jira Cloud spec completeness (an open question the plan itself flags) and still wouldn't pin the FAKE. The chosen shared-contract-body approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on testability and robustness. Alternatives discarded — no finding.

COACHING FINDING (minor — fitness-for-purpose gap in the mechanism's DEFAULT-suite leg):
- [ ] Make the "fake fails on divergence" mechanism non-vacuous in the default (hermetic) suite: name HOW the real-client shape is available to the assertion when the live leg is OFF (the default).
  - criteria: [G6]
  - location: "What" bullet 3 ("The fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's") and Acceptance Criteria line 2.
  - evidence: ["plan AC2: 'The fake fails the contract test if its return shape diverges from the real client's return shape.'", "real client runs ONLY behind the opt-in live/external marker per 'What' bullet 2 and AC1/AC4 — so in the default suite there is no live real-shape to diverge against, making AC2 enforceable only on the rare opt-in live run and weakening fitness-for-purpose", "tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py returns fixtures captured FROM the real client verbatim (NO shape massaging) — the hermetic divergence signal already exists, but the plan does not name the fixture-derived shape as the default-suite enforcement point"]
  - suggested_fix: State that the contract assertions run against the fake's fixture-derived output in the DEFAULT hermetic suite (where divergence surfaces immediately, since the fixtures are real captured payloads), and frame the live-gated leg as an ADDITIONAL freshness check that the fixtures still match live Jira — not the sole place AC2 is enforced.

(Note: the "Optional OpenAPI validation" bullet's external-feasibility concern is a T3 matter and is already tracked in this ticket's T3 audits; not re-filed under G6.)

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness) review.

Verdict: substantially a PASS. The chosen approach — a verified fake whose contract tests run against BOTH the real and fake client — is the canonical SWE-at-Google pattern and is the right fit for the named problem (preventing fixture/fake drift that reintroduced bug 0ee6). I generated two structural alternatives: (a) a JSON-schema/OpenAPI-only validation of the fake's outputs with no real-client run, and (b) a golden-snapshot diff of fake vs. recorded real responses. Both are strictly weaker: (a) drops the live cross-check that is the whole point of a *verified* fake; (b) is the change-detector anti-pattern the plan explicitly rejects. The plan's choice is defensibly at-least-as-good on all axes — DISCARD.

Two grounded coaching findings (both minor; do not block):

CRITERION G6 (mechanism correctness / fitness-for-purpose):
- [ ] Make explicit WHEN the promised drift is actually caught. The fake returns story-A fixtures verbatim (`tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/_fakes.py` returns `copy.deepcopy` with no reshaping), and those fixtures were captured FROM the real client. So the fake cannot fail a *shape* contract via its own code — the only thing that surfaces "a future real-API change" is the live-gated run, which is opt-in and won't execute in the default suite. The plan should state that fixture-staleness detection depends on periodically running the live arm (and/or re-capturing fixtures), so reviewers don't expect default-CI to catch real-API drift.
  evidence: plan "a future real-API change surfaces as a fake-drift failure" vs. AC "Running against live is opt-in (does not flake or slow the default suite)"; `_fakes.py:53-62` returns fixtures with no shape massaging.

CRITERION G6 (mechanism correctness — concrete codebase fact):
- [ ] Name the ACTUAL pytest marker. The plan says "behind the existing live/external pytest marker", but no `live` marker is registered — `pyproject.toml:327` registers `external` (third-party live services) and the repo runs under `--strict-markers --strict-config` (`pyproject.toml:326`), so an unregistered `@pytest.mark.live` is a hard collection error. State that the live arm uses the existing `external` marker (and lives under `tests/external/`, per the `pytest_collection_modifyitems` confinement in `tests/conftest.py`).
  evidence: ABSENCE of a `live` marker in registered markers; `pyproject.toml:326-336`; `tests/conftest.py:63` external-tier confinement.
  suggested_fix: replace "behind the existing live/external pytest marker" with "behind the existing `external` marker (test resides under `tests/external/`)".

Affirmations (criteria this chunk passes): G6 mechanism is logic/data-flow complete for the four enrichment methods, all of which exist on the real surface (`search_issues`, `acli_graph.get_comment_map`/`get_issuelinks_map`/`get_parent_map`); the structure/type-not-value assertion design correctly avoids the change-detector anti-pattern; scope cleanly excludes story A/B; and the plan states a clear POSITIVE rationale ("A fake is only as good as its fidelity… verified fake… same tests run against BOTH").

comments.body: Plan-review PASS (0 blocking, 3 advisory). Remediating: (1) the shape contract ENUMERATES the required keys per enrichment method (search_issues: each issue has key:str + fields:dict; get_comment_map: {key: {comments:list}}; get_issuelinks_map: {key: [{type:{name:str}, in/outwardIssue?}]}; get_parent_map: {key: str|None}) — checked via shared assertion fns; (2) DROPPING the optional OpenAPI leg — the untracked epic open question is resolved as out-of-scope: the dual-run real-vs-fake shape comparison IS the verified-fake honesty mechanism (SWE-at-Google), OpenAPI is YAGNI for a single team. Dual-run: identical contract fns parametrized over the fake (always, hermetic) + the real AcliClient (live-gated by REBAR_RUN_EXTERNAL + creds).

compiled_state.description:
## Why

A fake is only as good as its fidelity to the real client. If `FakeAcliClient` (story A) drifts from the real `AcliClient`, we reintroduce bug 0ee6's failure mode — tests pass against fictional shapes. Google's "Software Engineering at Google" prescribes the **verified fake**: the fake carries contract tests written against the API's public interface, and those same tests run against BOTH the real implementation and the fake.

## What

- **Shape-contract tests** for the `AcliClient` enrichment surface (`get_comment_map`, `get_issuelinks_map`, `get_parent_map`, `search_issues`) asserting the RETURN SHAPE (keys present, JSON primitive types) — not exact values.
- A parametrized harness that runs the identical contract tests against (a) the `FakeAcliClient` (always, hermetic) and (b) the real `AcliClient` against live Jira (behind the existing live/external pytest marker, opt-in).
- The fake FAILS the contract if its return shape diverges from the real client's, so a future real-API change surfaces as a fake-drift failure.
- Optional: validate the real client's responses against Jira Cloud's published OpenAPI where the spec covers the field (investigate spec completeness first — see epic open question).

## Scope

- In: the dual-run (real + fake) shape-contract harness for the enrichment methods.
- Out: capturing the fixtures (story A); the snapshot round-trip (story B).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shape-contract tests for the enrichment methods run against the fake (always) and the real client (live-gated marker).
- [ ] The fake fails the contract test if its return shape diverges from the real client's return shape.
- [ ] Assertions check structure/type (keys + JSON primitive types), not exact values (anti-change-detector).
- [ ] Running against live is opt-in (does not flake or slow the default suite).

file_impact.reason: shared return-shape contract for the enrichment surface (run against both impls)
organize the jira contract suite into a subdirectory

compiled_state.title: Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient

verify_commands.dd_text: The fake honours the enrichment-surface shape contract (hermetic) and a divergent fake fails it
The real AcliClient honours the same contract (opt-in, non-vacuous)
